refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, these should be separated into distinct sub-FRs. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain representation, which is a fundamental aspect of worksite area analysis and remains solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating terrain with relevant site features and safety zones
Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational policy overlays
Produce an abstracted spatial representation supporting dynamic updates and integration with planning modules
selected_dp.selected_dp: Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational policy overlays

potential_sources: Integration of terrain data and operational policies may create dependencies if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: By modularizing terrain representation and policy overlays, the design supports independence and easier updates, reducing coupling risks.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain data for modeling
Risk that operational policies may frequently change requiring flexible overlays
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume the modular worksite model format
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the modular boundaries between terrain representation and policy overlays to ensure independent updates and reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the integration and processing of environmental and operational data to create a modular worksite model that combines terrain data with operational policy overlays.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives terrain and sensor data relevant to the worksite environment.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational policies and task directives to overlay on the terrain model.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates data exchange with other system modules and external data sources.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the module is designed to integrate and model environmental and policy data; minimal diffusion expected outside this module.
Vehicle module primarily focuses on vehicle control and dynamics; containing the worksite model here would cause significant diffusion to other modules.
Teleoperation involves remote control and monitoring; partial relevance but poor containment of terrain and policy modeling.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety protocols; containing the worksite model here is inappropriate and would cause high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP as it is specifically designed to handle integration of terrain and operational policy data into a modular worksite model, ensuring high cohesion and low diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data feeds may require careful interface management to avoid coupling with other modules.
Changes in operational policies might require frequent updates to the module, impacting stability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying a modular worksite model that combines terrain data with operational policy overlays, directly fulfilling the requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data feeds may require careful interface management to avoid coupling with other modules.
Changes in operational policies might require frequent updates to the module, impacting stability.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion scores, indicating effective containment of the DP within a single module. Other modules have poor containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module receives telemetry and tasking inputs and produces a worksite model/map available to planning and execution functions, aligning with acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate and module boundary are clearly defined, with the NGHE Solution module responsible for integrating terrain and policy data, ensuring clear allocation of the FR to the IM.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface contracts to manage real-time data dependencies and minimize coupling risks.
Establish a change management process for operational policy updates to maintain module stability.
Consider monitoring diffusion metrics periodically to detect any unintended spread of DP responsibilities outside the NGHE Solution module.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear logical mapping, effective containment, and feasible verification. The NGHE Solution module adequately addresses the FR with high cohesion and low diffusion, and ownership boundaries are well defined. Although there are some diffusion risks related to real-time data dependencies and policy updates, these are manageable through recommended revisions such as interface contract documentation and change management processes. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to maintain the current level and focus on implementing these controls rather than decomposing further at this stage.

revise.why: To explicitly document interface contracts for real-time data dependencies and establish change management processes for operational policy updates, thereby minimizing coupling risks and maintaining module stability.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can be effectively maintained and updated as operational policies evolve.
Interface contracts can be clearly defined and enforced to manage dependencies.
open_risks: Potential coupling due to real-time data feed dependencies.
Frequent updates to operational policies impacting module stability.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming the NGHE Solution module produces accurate worksite models/maps.
Metrics monitoring diffusion scores over time to detect any spread of DP responsibilities.
